title: libpod/container_internal: Rename cleanupCgroups to cleanupResourceLimits
body:
This was recently split over OSes in b2df0dd1 (changes to allow for darwin compilation, 2018-06-20, #1015).  But cleanupCgroups is never going to implemented outside of Linux.  This commit switches to an OS-agnostic name that may eventually be implemented for the other OSes.

Spun off from [here][1].  I'll rebase once #1015 is cherry-picked into master.

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1015#discussion_r198647744

comments.body: Ok, #1015 has landed, so I've rebased onto master.  I've also moved the ignored error logic which landed in 7fc1a329 (#942) down into the Linux-specific helper.

I think that assuming the behavior change in Stop is sufficient to solve
CGroup cleanup issues is probably overly optimistic - it won't affect the
fact that we're failing to clean up CGroups because the conmon CGroup is
not empty.

I would actually like to remove this logic entirely and see about getting a
CGroup layout more similar to CRI-O where no cleanup logic is required.
That could be a week or so in coming though.

On Sat, Jun 30, 2018, 09:51 W. Trevor King <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *@wking* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In libpod/container_internal_linux.go
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1032#discussion_r199323085>:
>
> > @@ -39,7 +39,10 @@ func (c *Container) cleanupCgroups() error {
>  	}
>
>  	if err := cgroup.Delete(); err != nil {
> -		return err
> +		// For now we are going to only warn on failures to clean up cgroups
> +		// We have a conflict with running podman containers cleanup in same cgroup as container
> +		logrus.Warnf("Ignoring Error cleaning up container %s CGroups: %v", c.ID(), err)
> +		return nil
>
> You changed the behaviour.
>
> Did I? In master, this error causes cleanupCgroups to error
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/b96be3af1b9d00662758211420c955becbaf2f9e/libpod/container_internal_linux.go#L42>,
> which we then ignore
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/b96be3af1b9d00662758211420c955becbaf2f9e/libpod/container_internal.go#L899>.
> Now we ignore this error and return early here. I think those are the same.
>
> My new code is making *other* errors from this helper fatal again (e.g. this
> one
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/b96be3af1b9d00662758211420c955becbaf2f9e/libpod/container_internal_linux.go#L38>),
> but I didn't think #942 <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/942>
> cared whether they were fatal or not. It only spoke about failed removal.
>
> Now we do not cleanup storage if the cgroup cleanup fails.
>
> I think we will for cgroup.Delete errors.
>
> Why did you make this change?
>
> Because
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1032#issuecomment-401479031>
> ignoring cgroup-deletion errors is, like the rest of our cgroup logic,
> Linux-specific.
>
> You could test the theory by...
>
> I don't have a working podman locally, but I'll see what I can find in
> the CI logs.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1032#discussion_r199323085>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCE-y8_njNMJFsqV9wxLhGe8fCop1ks5uB4JGgaJpZM4U9oi3>
> .
>

>  I would actually like to remove this logic entirely and see about getting a CGroup layout more similar to CRI-O...

Sounds good to me, but in the meantime we need to ignore [these][1], right?


[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/942#issuecomment-400460083

Yes. I have no objections to this approach as a stopgap until we can get a
more permanent solution written.

On Sat, Jun 30, 2018, 10:31 W. Trevor King <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> I would actually like to remove this logic entirely and see about getting
> a CGroup layout more similar to CRI-O...
>
> Sounds good to me, but in the meantime we need to ignore these

I think I've seen this type of test failure before, and they were why I ended up introducing a bool to track whether CGroups have been created - opening CGroups will sometimes hang under unclear circumstances. I resolved it by only attempting to open the CGroups when I knew they were present (the bool was set) - I'm not sure what's broken it here.
@wking Can you rebase to pick up the fix for the Travis tests?

> Can you rebase to pick up the fix for the Travis tests?

Done with 50182c85 -> a4b6f008 (Git reported no conflicts).

> The latest upstream changes (presumably cf2be66) made this pull request unmergeable.

Hmm.  This PR [was approved][1] before #1051 [was][2], but #1051 ended up [landing first][3], and with #1051 we don't want this PR anymore.  How do we get it out of the merge queue?

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1032#issuecomment-402987071
[2]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1051#issuecomment-403044301
[3]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1051#event-1720599644
I think we can close it safely, Homu won't merge with conflicts
